mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports near-term equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns remain negative or flat during periods of sustained industrial production growth, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.3 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where inflation prints above 0.3 percent, or the signal shows no predictive edge after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending trajectories and corporate earnings revisions that generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day equity returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals acute manufacturing weakness and potential supply-side bottlenecks, prompting market participants to price in earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where industrial production falls by more than 0.5 percent, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive value over baseline momentum.
